description: Curate dedup/enrich leave orphan 130-byte ai-title session files in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<uuid>.jsonl despite --no-session-persistence (Claude Code 2.1.110 bug). Fix: force a deterministic --session-id per curate spawn and detach a 30s-delayed cleanup thread that deletes that exact file. Loops are unchanged.

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to callers that pass the existing 9-arg signature — callers must be explicitly updated to pass the new flag

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Each task delivers working code + tests together
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Test boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
A cleanup mechanism that could delete unrelated session files (e.g., interactive sessions, other curate batches, loop iterations)
A cleanup thread that blocks or prolongs curate latency
CLI arg ordering that breaks --no-session-persistence (must stay before -p per learning 671)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: spawn_claude signature gains a 10th positional arg `cleanup_title_artifact: bool` at the end
Positive: When cleanup_title_artifact=true, argv contains exactly one `--session-id <valid-uuid-v4>` pair positioned after --model/--effort and before `-p`
Positive: When cleanup_title_artifact=false, argv contains NO `--session-id` flag (unchanged from current behavior)
Positive: The cleanup thread is spawned AFTER cmd.spawn() succeeds (not before — we don't want dangling threads if spawn itself fails)
Positive: The cleanup thread is DETACHED (no join handle stored; the function returns immediately regardless of thread state)
Positive: Existing loop-engine and learnings-ingestion callers pass `false` and produce byte-identical argv to today
Negative: No code path forces --session-id when cleanup_title_artifact=false
Negative: The cleanup thread must NOT scan, glob, or enumerate the projects directory — it must `remove_file` a single, deterministic path
Negative: spawn_claude must not block on the cleanup thread (no join(), no channel.recv())
Unit test: `encoded_cwd_dir(Path::new('$HOME/foo'), Path::new('$HOME'))` returns `$HOME/.claude/projects/-home-chris-foo`
Unit test: `encoded_cwd_dir(Path::new('$HOME/projects/task-mgr'), Path::new('$HOME'))` returns `$HOME/.claude/projects/-home-chris-Documents-startat0-Projects-task-mgr`
Unit test (via mock CLAUDE_BINARY that echoes argv): passing cleanup_title_artifact=true inserts `--session-id <uuid>` between --model and -p; passing false produces argv with no --session-id
Unit test: uuid produced is a valid v4 (version nibble == 4) — parse it with Uuid::parse_str and assert get_version() == Some(Version::Random)
Unit test: filename encoding handles the trailing-slash cwd case — `$HOME/foo/` encodes identically to `$HOME/foo` (strip trailing slash before encoding)

Known-bad: if the implementation puts --session-id AFTER -p, the flag is ignored by Claude and the cleanup path has a mismatched UUID → file never gets deleted. Test: assert argv index of '--session-id' < argv index of '-p'.
Known-bad: if the implementation uses std::fs::read_dir + filter to find 'recent' files, test that creates an UNRELATED recent .jsonl in the target dir and verifies it SURVIVES the cleanup.
Failure mode: if HOME env var is unset, spawn_claude still returns Ok — cleanup is best-effort; the spawn itself must not fail because cleanup could not be scheduled
Failure mode: if the target file never appears (Claude crashed before writing title), remove_file returns NotFound and the thread exits silently
userStories.description:
Extend spawn_claude in src/loop_engine/claude.rs with a new `cleanup_title_artifact: bool` argument (appended to the end of the positional args to match the existing pattern; the function is already under #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]).

When `cleanup_title_artifact` is true:
1. Generate `uuid::Uuid::new_v4()` once per invocation.
2. Insert `--session-id <uuid>` into the argv. Place it after `--model`/`--effort` but BEFORE `-p` (learning 671: flag ordering matters — flags before -p).
3. After `cmd.spawn()` succeeds, compute the target jsonl path:
   - effective_cwd = working_dir.map(PathBuf::from).unwrap_or_else(|| std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_default())
   - Canonicalize it so symlinks (e.g. Dropbox → Documents/startat0 symlink in this repo) resolve to the same path Claude encodes
   - encoded = effective_cwd.to_string_lossy().replace('/', '-')   // e.g. '$HOME/foo' → '-home-chris-foo'
   - home = std::env::var('HOME')? ; projects_dir = home/.claude/projects/<encoded>
   - target = projects_dir.join(format!('{}.jsonl', uuid))
4. Spawn a DETACHED cleanup thread: `std::thread::spawn(move || { std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(30)); let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&target); });`
5. Do NOT join the thread. Do NOT block curate on it.

When `cleanup_title_artifact` is false (default): behavior is identical to today — no UUID, no --session-id, no thread.

DO:
- Pull the cleanup delay into a named const (e.g. `const TITLE_ARTIFACT_CLEANUP_DELAY: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);`) for later tuning.
- Extract path-encoding into a small pure helper `fn encoded_cwd_dir(cwd: &Path, home: &Path) -> PathBuf` so it can be unit-tested without spawning.
- Log nothing on success (silent cleanup is correct behavior); optionally log at debug/trace level only.
- Pass `--session-id` only when the opt-in is true — do not set it unconditionally, as real loop sessions should still get auto-generated IDs.

DO NOT:
- Do not thread-join in the spawning function (defeats the purpose).
- Do not use any snapshot/diff approach to find the file — the UUID makes the filename deterministic, which is the whole point.
- Do not add a global cleanup that scans the projects directory — risks deleting real sessions.
- Do not set --session-id when `cleanup_title_artifact=false` — loops should keep today's behavior exactly.
- Do not reuse `cleanup_ghost_sessions()` at src/loop_engine/claude.rs:668 — that targets ~/.claude/sessions/, a DIFFERENT directory; the orphan title files land in ~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/ instead.

Edge cases:
- HOME unset: skip cleanup (return Ok, don't error the spawn).
- current_dir() fails: skip cleanup (same).
- File doesn't exist at 30s: remove_file returns NotFound — swallow.
- Claude process still running at 30s: irrelevant, file is only ever a single-line ai-title; deleting during a future write would be a race, but Claude writes the title once during init, so the 30s delay means we delete after the write is done in practice.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Inserting --session-id AFTER -p would pass compilation but Claude parses flags left-of-prompt only; the flag would be ignored and a fresh UUID chosen, making the cleanup path wrong.
- Using thread::spawn without `move` and capturing a non-'static reference — won't compile.
- Holding the `working_dir: Option<&Path>` borrow into the cleanup thread — must be converted to an owned PathBuf before the thread is spawned.
edgeCases: HOME env var unset: skip scheduling cleanup, return Ok from spawn
working_dir=None and std::env::current_dir() errors: skip cleanup, return Ok
working_dir=Some(tmpdir) (used by loop in worktrees): encode using that dir, not process cwd
cwd with trailing slash: normalize by trimming trailing '/' before encoding
Claude process exits before 30s: cleanup thread still runs, file exists, gets deleted normally
Claude process takes >30s to write title: cleanup removes nothing that run, file remains; next curate run with same UUID is impossible (UUIDs are unique)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Skip cleanup scheduling; spawn still succeeds; no error surfaced to caller
Thread exits silently; no log noise
Thread exits silently; orphan survives but is inert

failureModes.cause: Process panics mid-spawn before thread is spawned
failureModes.expectedBehavior: No thread was ever spawned (cleanup is scheduled AFTER child.spawn() succeeds), so no dangling work

userStories.notes:
Key file: src/loop_engine/claude.rs (spawn_claude at line 80, args-building block at lines 92-144, Command spawn at line 149-187).

Learning [671]: CLI arg ordering matters — flags before -p. Place --session-id in the flag group.

Existing cleanup precedent (different dir): src/loop_engine/claude.rs:668 `cleanup_ghost_sessions()` targets ~/.claude/sessions/ but orphan title files are in ~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/ — a different path. Do not conflate.

UUID dep already in Cargo.toml: `uuid = { version = '1', features = ['v4', 'serde'] }` — already used in src/commands/curate/dedup.rs and src/commands/curate/enrich.rs.

Existing test mutex: src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs::CLAUDE_BINARY_MUTEX serializes tests that mutate CLAUDE_BINARY. Use it in new tests.

Path encoding evidence (from `ls ~/.claude/projects/`): `$HOME/foo/bar` → `-home-chris-foo-bar`. No fancy escaping — just `/` → `-`. Leading slash becomes a leading dash.

Canonicalization note: this repo's cwd can be reached as both `$HOME/projects/task-mgr` (symlink) and `$HOME/projects/task-mgr` (real). Claude encodes whichever path the process runs under, so just use the cwd as-is without resolving symlinks — resolution would break if Claude used the un-resolved form.

Mock pattern for argv tests: tests/fixtures/fake_claude.sh already exists and echoes argv. Set CLAUDE_BINARY=<script> via env in a test while holding CLAUDE_BINARY_MUTEX.

correctness: Thread is detached — no join, no channel, no handle stored
Cleanup targets exactly one deterministic path; no enumeration of the projects directory
When cleanup_title_artifact=false, zero behavior change for existing callers (loops + ingestion)
performance: spawn_claude return time must not increase — cleanup thread spawns in O(1) and does not wait
No allocations in the hot path of the flag check when cleanup_title_artifact=false
style: Extract the path-encoding into a named pure function for testability
Put the 30s delay behind a const for tunability
Match the existing clap-style arg-pushing pattern (args.push(flag.to_string()); args.push(value.to_string()))
Preserve the current `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` since signature grows by one

userStories.title: Add opt-in --session-id + deferred cleanup to spawn_claude

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: src/commands/curate/mod.rs:604 dedup call passes `true` as the 10th arg
Positive: src/commands/curate/enrich.rs:235 enrich call passes `true` as the 10th arg
Positive: All other callers (ingestion, prd_reconcile, engine, watchdog tests, claude.rs tests) pass `false`
Positive: cargo check passes with zero 'missing argument' errors
Negative: No caller passes `Some(true)`, `Default::default()`, or any indirection — the bool is a bare literal at each call site
Negative: learnings::ingestion::extract_learnings_from_output must still pass `false` (out of scope)
Unit/integration test: existing curate tests still pass (they use mock CLAUDE_BINARY, so the cleanup thread tries to delete a file that doesn't exist — remove_file returns NotFound, swallowed)

Manual verification (documented in progress.txt): running `CLAUDE_BINARY=/tmp/claude-log-argv.sh cargo run --release -- curate dedup --dry-run --concurrency 1` logs argv containing `--session-id <uuid>` before `-p`
userStories.description:
Update every caller of spawn_claude to pass the new 10th arg explicitly. The two curate call sites pass `true`; all other callers pass `false` to preserve current behavior.

Call sites (from grep of `spawn_claude\(`):
- src/commands/curate/mod.rs:604 (dedup)                    → PASS TRUE
- src/commands/curate/enrich.rs:235 (enrich)               → PASS TRUE
- src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs:84 (loop-side extraction) → PASS FALSE (scope says curate only; not touching ingestion here)
- src/loop_engine/prd_reconcile.rs:659                     → PASS FALSE
- src/loop_engine/engine.rs:528 (main loop)                → PASS FALSE
- src/loop_engine/watchdog.rs:308, 364 (tests)             → PASS FALSE
- src/loop_engine/claude.rs:747 + test sites 1013/1087/1491 → PASS FALSE

DO:
- Update exactly these 2 curate call sites to `true`.
- Update every other call site to an explicit `false` — compile errors force you to visit all of them; do not bypass with a default.
- Keep permission_mode, model, effort, allowed-tools arguments unchanged at the curate sites.

DO NOT:
- Do not ship a default value for the new arg (no `Option<bool>`, no Default trait) — the positional arg pattern used by spawn_claude requires every caller to be explicit.
- Do not enable cleanup in learnings/ingestion, loop, or prd_reconcile in this task — user scope is curate only. (Follow-up task can consider ingestion separately.)

Known-bad to guard against:
- Missing a caller → compile error. Good. Don't paper over with `_ = cleanup_title_artifact` shims.
edgeCases: Test call sites in watchdog.rs and claude.rs — must be updated too or tests won't compile

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compile error identifies it immediately; fix and retry

userStories.notes: This is mechanical once FEAT-001 lands. Use the grep pattern `spawn_claude\(` to find every call site. Expect ~8 sites to touch in src/ and 4-5 in test code.

correctness: Every caller is explicitly updated; compile errors drive completeness
Only curate sites flip to true
style: Match existing trailing-comma-and-newline formatting at each call site

userStories.title: Flip opt-in at curate call sites; keep all other callers on false

acceptanceCriteria: Path-encoding helper is a pure function, not a method on spawn_claude
30s delay lives in a named const
No code duplication between the flag-push block and the thread-spawn block
spawn_claude remains under a reasonable size after the change (flag the function if it grew beyond 300 lines)
userStories.description:
Review FEAT-001 and FEAT-002 changes for DRY, complexity, and coupling. Specifically check: is the path-encoding helper reusable for a future ingestion opt-in? Is the 30s constant easy to change? Is the cleanup-thread pattern small enough that a second opt-in site wouldn't duplicate code?

DO: Check for duplicated logic, functions >30 lines, tight coupling.
DO NOT: Expand scope to other callers; this is review-only.

userStories.description:
Review all implementation for correctness, security, integration wiring, and documentation.

DO:
- Verify argv order via a mock CLAUDE_BINARY wrapper that logs argv — confirm `--session-id <uuid>` appears before `-p`.
- Run a real `task-mgr curate dedup --dry-run --concurrency 1` (quick, read-only) and confirm: (a) files appear briefly in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/, (b) within ~35s they are gone.
- Check `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean.
- Review spawn_claude doc comment: the description of base flags must mention --session-id opt-in.
- Check CLAUDE.md — if a 'curate session cleanup' section is warranted for future contributors, add one (brief, under 10 lines).

DO NOT:
- Skip the empirical cleanup-verification step — this is the whole point of the feature; unit tests alone don't prove the file gets deleted in the real projects dir.
userStories.notes: If issues found: add FIX-xxx tasks to JSON (priority 50-97), commit.
